title: R Booster
text:
Preface
This is a Quarto book on R.
To learn more about Quarto books visit https://quarto.org/docs/books.

title: Reproducibility
text:
Working with renv to improve reproducibility. This needs to happen before any packages are installed, otherwise participants don’t get to explore the empty-ish lock.file.
See:https://lennartwittkuhn.com/repro-book/chapters/renv.html

title: Package Management
text:
Functions to:

install packages
load packages
unload packages
update packages
delete packages

Use the annotater to annotate package load calls with version numbers, description, functions used.
Use session_info() to see what’s loaded, as well as other information* (should session_info come elsewhere? documentation maybe?)

title: Project Setup & Environment
text:
Environment alternative subsection title: “Blank Slates”
alternative chapter names: “Getting Started”, “Setup”
When you quit R, do not save the workspace to an .Rdata file. When you launch, do not reload the workspace from an .Rdata file. This ensures a blank slate everytime you start a new R session.
In RStudio, you can set this via Tools > Global Options.

text:

Creating Projects
Opening Projects

title: Project Setup & Environment
text:
Paths
We don’t want setwd("path/that/only/works/on/my/machine")
RStudio projects automatically sets the working directory for us.

Explain Absolute Paths
Explain Relative Paths

title: Configuration
text:
Configuration Files
Set up configuration files to carry out certain operations like dropping columns, renaming columns, recoding values - as opposed to hard-coding things in the script.

title: Configuration
text:
Working With Multiple Files
importing single vs. multiple files (lists) is mentioned in this section of our brainstorm document, but don’t know if it belongs here or elsewhere.

title: Working with APIs
text: Use the DataverseNL API to fetch data for this workshop.

title: Data Pipeline
text:
Explore Data
Validate Data
Drop Columns
Split and/or Merge Columns
Rename Columns
Adjust Variable Types
Filter outliers and/or missing data
De-identify data
Standardize values (dates, country, units)
Recoding
Construct new variables

From our brainstorm notes:

Rename Columns
Adjust number of cases
De-identify data
Drop any irrelevant columns
Split columns as needed
Rename variables
Transform/normalize variables
Standardize variables
Update any variable types as needed
Recode variables
Construct any necessary variables
Add missing values as needed
Add metadata as needed
Validate data
Merge and/or append data as needed
Transform data as needed

title: Documentation
text:
Create a codebook using the codebook package
Use a README template, add session_info() to the README and maybe automate it being updated.
CITATION.cff
CHANGELOG (optional) and/or data cleaning checklist or plan (optional)

title: Automation
text: Demonstrate how you could automate scripts - use a controller script or batch file / shell script. - schedule the scripts to run at a specific time or interval - say something about SNAKEMAKE? Or the targets package? Might be overkill..

title: Help
text:
How to use ?function to search for the function documentation
See the “Teach Me How To Google” presentation for tips on searching for errors and issues
how to create reproducicble examples..reprexes
LLM assistance: do not paste your data into the LLM chatbots! use reprexes instead. Many tidyverse packages now have an LLM helper trained on the (pacakge documentation) on their websites. Use Mistral or Lumo (EU-based, privacy-focus), EduGenAI is also coming up. Try to use the Study Mode option so you don’t get the answers immediately to copy and paste, but ask it to help figure things out.
